title: tenants — third-party subprocessors
description: Third-party services this module uses. Aggregated at repo root by `tenancy:snapshot-subprocessors` into a public subprocessor registry (GDPR Art. 28 requirement — every enterprise DPA lists our subprocessors). Every entry names the purpose, region, DPA link, and data classes touched. Removing a subprocessor requires 30d advance notice to tenants per most DPA templates.

subprocessors.name: AWS Route53
subprocessors.purpose: DNS lookups for custom domain verification (VerifyDomainDnsJob). We resolve customer DNS through Route53 recursive resolvers.

subprocessors.name: AWS KMS
subprocessors.purpose: Encrypts TenantIntegration.config at rest. Crypto-shredding on right-to-erasure = destroy the KMS key.

subprocessors.name: Cloudflare
subprocessors.purpose: SSL/TLS termination for custom domains + edge caching for the tenant picker central host.
subprocessors.note: TLS terminates at Cloudflare. Origin-to-Cloudflare is re-encrypted end-to-end (full-strict mode).

subprocessors.name: SendGrid
subprocessors.purpose: Transactional email delivery for tenants notifications (welcome, suspension, domain-verified).
subprocessors.region: us + eu (data residency configurable per Application)

subprocessors.name: Sentry
subprocessors.purpose: Error monitoring + release tracking. Errors from this module route here.
subprocessors.region: eu (frankfurt) for EU-Application deployments; us (san-francisco) otherwise
subprocessors.scrubbing: PII scrubbing enabled at the SDK level — data-classes.json restricted fields never enter Sentry payloads.

subprocessors.name: Segment
subprocessors.purpose: Analytics pipeline sink. analytics.json events land here first, then fan out to downstream tools.
subprocessors.region: us (dublin egress mirror available for EU tenants)
subprocessors.note: Marketing-tier events only fire with `consent_tier=marketing` consent captured (analytics.json). Essential events fire unconditionally per ePrivacy Directive.
